dimensions.note: Workflow-scope report. It consumes the upstream @jentz/aws-vpc-inventory step's vpc and scan_error artifacts already persisted in swamp (read via context.dataRepository.getContent) and makes no AWS calls of its own, so it handles no credentials or secrets. No import of any AWS SDK or credential provider.

info on run start (workflow), on collection completion (vpcs/errors/steps/skipped counts), and on finish (row count + degraded suffix); warn on an undecodable artifact, a schema-failed vpc row (with the failing field paths), and when steps exist but none match the expected modelType (logging the observed types). All use structured {placeholder} props and no secrets. Logging is routed through tryLog, which swallows logger errors so observability never affects correctness — appropriate for a never-throws report.

The report never throws by design. collect decodes each artifact defensively: bad JSON (decodeJson returns undefined) and schema-mismatched rows (VpcRecordSchema/ScanErrorSchema safeParse failure) are counted into skipped and dropped, never thrown. report.execute wraps generatedAt/collect/renderMarkdown in a try/catch whose only effect on failure is to set degraded=true, log a warn, and substitute a minimal still-valid markdown body — so a hostile context (e.g. a throwing stepExecutions getter) degrades rather than failing the workflow. This trades a hard failure for a degraded flag deliberately: a thrown report would lose the rich JSON payload swamp persists, and the degraded flag plus skipped count surface the problem to the operator.

tests/aws_vpc_inventory_report_test.ts is the exhaustive matrix: compareVpcs ordering; collect decoding (matching step, unrelated model type ignored, bad-JSON skipped, schema-mismatch skipped, unrelated spec ignored-not-skipped); renderMarkdown (empty collection, stable sort with default/shared markers, the auth_expired / access_denied / other coverage-gap sections); and report.execute JSON payload (structured vpcs[] rows in sort order, errorsByKind + skipped, empty context degraded=false, a throwing-getter context degraded=true, and an explicit assertion the payload carries NO csv/columns keys and the markdown has no CSV recipe). The colocated aws_vpc_inventory_report_test.ts pins manifest/name sync, scope === 'workflow', and the never-throws empty-context path. Both success and failure/degraded paths are covered.

Pure read-and-render with no persisted resources and no external mutation; re-running over the same step data yields the same markdown + JSON. Resilient to missing upstream steps (empty report), undecodable/schema-drifted artifacts (skip-and-count), method-arg-only spec resolution (handle.metadata?.tags?.specName ?? handle.specName), and an outright collection failure (degraded fallback). Deterministic compareVpcs sort makes the row order stable across runs.

Makes no external HTTP/AWS calls. Its only contract is the upstream @jentz/aws-vpc-inventory vpc and scan_error row shapes, which it re-validates with Zod safeParse against a hand-mirrored copy of the producer's VpcRecordSchema/ScanErrorSchema before use — so producer-side schema tightening surfaces as a skipped artifact rather than a bad read.

Creates no cloud resources, network connections, file handles, or temp files. Reads stored bytes via context.dataRepository.getContent, decodes with a shared TextDecoder, and returns markdown + JSON; nothing to clean up or track.

README.md contains zero 12-digit account-id literals and zero IPv4 address literals (grep-verified); the workflow YAML example uses placeholder workflow/model/report names and the jq example uses a generic report-name. Test fixtures use synthetic repeated-digit account ids (111111111111, 999999999999, 222, 999), placeholder profile/region/vpc identifiers, and RFC1918 CIDRs. No real IPs, internal hostnames, account ids, or ARNs tied to real infrastructure.
